feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Issue with S4 app - Schedule Order Acknowledgement output for Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Hello Team, We are currently testing the S/4HANA app “Schedule Order Acknowledgement Output” for Purchase Orders, specifically for the Order Acknowledgement Reminder scenario.

As per the Confirmation Control configuration, the setup is as follows:

  • Monitoring period: 2 days
  • Reference date: PO date + monitoring time
  • “Subject to Reminder” checkbox: Enabled

Based on this configuration, we need to understand the application behavior for the following points:

  1. Reminder Triggering Logic
    We expect to receive reminders until the AB Order Acknowledgement confirmation is received from the supplier. However, we observe inconsistent behavior:

    • For some POs, reminders are not received at all
    • For other POs, reminders are received daily

  2. PO Selection Criteria
    We would like to understand how the app selects Purchase Orders for sending reminders.

  3. Reminder Stop Condition
    Our understanding is that reminders should stop only after the AB Order Acknowledgement confirmation is received from the supplier.
    Please clarify:

    • Is this the only stop condition?
    • Is there a way to restrict or stop reminders when no AB Order Acknowledgement is received from the supplier over a certain period?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Combined Credit/Debit Note Posting Against Multiple Invoices for Customer Rate Difference
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently using the Customer Rate Difference Bills Auto Debit process in SAP GROW / SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

The existing process is working as expected. However, we have a new business requirement for selected customers.

Current Process:
Credit notes and debit notes are generated based on POAM / rate difference adjustments, and the current SAP GROW process is functioning correctly.

New Requirement:
For a few customers, instead of posting separate credit and debit notes invoice-wise, we need the system to calculate the combined total amount across all relevant credit and debit adjustments and then post only one consolidated credit note or debit note against multiple invoices.

For example:

  • Multiple invoices may have credit note and/or debit note adjustments.
  • The system should total all applicable credit and debit values for the customer.
  • Based on the net amount:
    • If the final amount is credit, create one consolidated credit note.
    • If the final amount is debit, create one consolidated debit note.
  • The document should still have reference/linkage to the multiple original invoices.

Business Need:
This is required for specific customers who do not want multiple adjustment documents. They require a single consolidated debit/credit note for all applicable invoices.

Questions for SAP:

  1. Is this requirement supported in standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition / GROW with SAP?
  2. Can one credit memo or debit memo be created with reference to multiple invoices in this scenario?
  3. Is it possible to net both credit and debit adjustments and create only one final credit/debit memo document?
  4. If not available in standard, is there a recommended SAP-approved approach using:
    • Configuration,
    • Billing document split control,
    • Custom logic,
    • Key user extensibility,
    • Side-by-side extension,
    • Or another standard scope item?
  5. Are there any limitations related to billing type, document flow, tax calculation, accounting posting, invoice reference, or audit compliance for this requirement?

Expected Outcome:
whether this requirement is achievable in SAP GROW standard functionality or whether an enhancement/custom extension is required. If possible, please provide the recommended solution approach and relevant configuration/extensibility options.

entry.title: Issue Maintaining 19-Digit Bank Account Number for Customer Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are facing an issue in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud while maintaining customer bank details through the “Manage Business Partner Master Data” / "Maintain Business Partner" app.

The customer has a 19-digit bank account number, however, the system currently allows entry of only up to 18 digits for the bank account number field.

To resolve this, we tried updating the bank account number length from 18 to 19 in the country-specific settings (attached image for reference), but the change is not taking effect and the system is still restricting the entry to 18 digits.

Could you please suggest:

  1. Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to maintain a bank account number with more than 18 digits in the Business Partner app?
  2. Is there any configuration, SSCUI, or workaround available to achieve this requirement?
  3. Are there any SAP notes or standard limitations related to this behavior?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

 

Thanks and Regards,

Rohini Dutta

entry.title: Requirement for CDS View – India GST Tax Breakup
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We require a CDS view for India GST tax breakup, covering CGST, SGST, IGST, and UGST components. This is needed for the development of the Purchase Register and Sales Register for our client.

We request your support on this at the earliest.

Regards,

Mayur Patil.

entry.title: Which CDS view provides Commodity Code data for a product in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Commodity Code field is visible in the Manage Product Master Data app, but it is not clear which released CDS view exposes this data for consumption in custom reports or SD form templates (Sales Order, Outbound Delivery, Tax Invoice).

Which CDS view should be used to fetch Commodity Code (Customs Tariff Number) data for a product?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Cannot create Party Entitled to Dispose
entry.content:
I am creating a new company code and have gotten most of the steps complete. I am stuck on creating the Party Entitled to Dispose. I have created the Business Partner exactly the same as the business partner for the original company code. When I go to Set Warehouse Address and add the new warehouse in, I get the error message "The system can't determine the party entitled to dispose." 

Old Company code = 1010. New Company Code = 2010

Things I have checked: 

  • Warehouse is EWM Managed
  • Storage Locations are attached to warehouse
  • Company code 2010 exists and is active
  • Country, currency, and address are maintained
  • Fiscal year variant assigned
  • Posting period variant assigned
  • 2010 → same controlling area as 1010
  • Every plant used in logistics is assigned to 2010
  • No plant is left assigned to 1010 accidentally
  • No duplicate plant assignment across incompatible company codes
  • Storage locations exist under correct plant
  • PED exists for warehouse
  • PED is assigned to:
    • Warehouse number
    • Plant
    • Storage locations (if required)
  • PED is ACTIVE (not draft / inactive)
  • PED is linked to correct organizational unit (often shipping point or warehouse org unit)
  • BP has both Supplier and Customer role
  • Company code segment exists for 2010
  • Purchasing / shipping data is maintained
  • BP is not missing “plant-level” logistics roles

I know I am missing something obvious/simple but I just can't figure it out. Any help is greatly appreciated. 

Vicki

entry.title: How to reduce/adjust the excess depreciation posted in public cloud ?
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client has posted excess depreciation as per IT Act, Now how to reduce/adjust the excess depreciation posted, but not reverse the depreciation. 

 Manual Dep. Write up is to post additional depreciation, but to reduce the depreciation we don't have any option.

And we can't create new dep. keys in the public cloud system. 

Kindly advice. 

 

Thank You.

entry.title: Want some of tolerance in the CO11N after Production order is created
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a requirement to maintain tolerance limits for BOM components during Goods Receipt confirmation in CO11N. Currently, SAP allows users to manually change component quantities while posting GR for Production Orders of SFG/FG without any restriction.

Our requirement is to control these quantity changes within a defined tolerance limit. For example, if a BOM component quantity is maintained as 10 KG, the user should not be allowed to change it to an unrealistic quantity such as 100 KG during CO11N confirmation.

We need a restriction mechanism where users can modify component quantities only within a specified tolerance range, such as ±30% or ±50% of the original BOM quantity maintained for the component.

Please suggest the possible solution or approach that can be implemented for this scenario.

Thanks & Regards,
Anurag

entry.title: Multiple Cash Clearing Accounts with Single Account Symbol
entry.content:
Multiple Cash Clearing Accounts with Single Account Symbol in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

EBS Configuration for Japanese Consolidated Billing (締め請求)

Prerequisites

Hello experts,

This question is based on a Japan-specific business requirement: automatic clearing of open receivables during Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) processing for consolidated billing (締め請求). In this process, multiple invoices are aggregated monthly and settled as a single lump-sum payment. The requirement is to accurately auto-clear the corresponding open items during EBS processing.

Background

We are implementing Electronic Bank Statement processing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and encountering challenges with account symbol configuration in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings for multiple cash accounts.

Current Configuration

Item Detail

House Bank AccountJPDP1/1EG2 → assigned to G/L Account 11001011 (Ordinary Deposit 1)
Intended MappingCash clearing account linked to house bank G/L account 11001011: 11002080
ErrorThe account symbol +++++++080 in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings attempts to post to G/L account 11001080, but house bank account JPDP1/1EG2 is not assigned to G/L account 11001080, resulting in a posting error. We suspect that dynamic derivation based on the house bank account's G/L assignment is not functioning.

Expected Behavior

We expected that the account symbol +++++++080 in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings would dynamically resolve to the appropriate clearing account based on the G/L account assigned to the house bank account master data.

Questions

Q1: Dynamic Account Derivation Does SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition support dynamic derivation of the clearing account based on the house bank account's G/L assignment when using a single account symbol (+++++++080) in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings?

Q2: Configuration Options in Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings Are there any configuration options within the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings to support multiple cash clearing accounts — where each house bank account maps to a different clearing account?

Q3: Posting Rule Configuration and Automatic Clearing If we use Posting Rules to route postings to multiple cash clearing accounts, what is the correct configuration approach?

Additionally, we are experiencing the following issue:

  • Without Posting Rules configured: EBS processing correctly auto-clears open receivables during bank statement import
  • After configuring Posting Rules: Auto-clearing of open receivables during EBS processing no longer works

How should Posting Rules be configured to support multiple cash clearing account assignment while preserving automatic clearing for Japanese consolidated billing (締め請求) lump-sum payments?

Technical Details

Item Detail

SystemSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
ProcessElectronic Bank Statement Processing (Consolidated Billing / 締め請求)
ModuleCash Management
Configuration AppsSSCUI 101024 (Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings), SSCUI 103557 (Assign Account Symbols to Payment Methods)

Current Issue Summary

The account symbol +++++++080 in the Electronic Bank Statement Global Settings is attempting to post to G/L account 11001080 rather than dynamically resolving to the correct clearing account (11002080) based on the house bank account's G/L assignment (11001011), resulting in a posting error.

Furthermore, after configuring Posting Rules, automatic clearing of open receivables is no longer triggered during EBS processing, breaking the auto-clearing requirement for consolidated billing lump-sum payments.

We need to achieve both:

  1. Correct clearing account assignment per house bank account
  2. Automatic clearing of open receivables for consolidated billing payments

Any guidance on the correct configuration approach would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards,

Tamaki

entry.title: How to view Movement Type to Transaction Key mapping in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hello,
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, I need to create a document showing which accounting entries (GL accounts) are posted for each movement type used in MIGO transactions.
What I can currently see:

SSCUI 100297 (Automatic Account Determination) shows the mapping: Transaction Key + Valuation Class → GL Account

What I cannot find:

The mapping: Movement Type → Value String → Transaction Key

In On-Premise systems, this can be viewed via tables T156SY and T156W, but I cannot find an equivalent screen or app in Public Cloud.
My questions:

Is there a standard way to view the Movement Type → Transaction Key relationship in Public Cloud?
If not available in the UI, what is the recommended approach to document the complete posting logic (Movement Type → GL Accounts) for each goods movement?
Has anyone created such documentation by running test postings and reviewing FI documents, or is there a better method?

Any guidance would be appreciated.
Thanks!

entry.title: Bulk upload of Supplier Master Data using Fiori app in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Hello,

We have confirmed that the Business Partner API can be used to create supplier master data programmatically in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

However, our customer is asking if there is a Fiori app that allows bulk upload of supplier master data (similar to how "Import Supplier Invoices" app works for invoice data).

What we know so far:

Migration Cockpit can be used for initial data migration
Mass Maintenance apps can update existing Business Partners but cannot create new ones
Business Partner API is available for system-to-system integration
Our question: Is there a standard Fiori app in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that supports bulk creation of supplier master data via file upload (e.g., Excel template)?

If not, what is the recommended approach for regularly creating multiple suppliers in the production environment (outside of initial migration)?

Thank you!

entry.title: Whether it is possible to change the posting to the Main Bank G/L in Manage Automatic Payments
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

①Request:

Could you please confirm whether it is possible to change the posting G/L account to the Main Bank G/L in Manage Automatic Payments (F0770)? If it is possible, please advise the required settings and any potential system impacts.

②Background:

The customer requires that the payment document posted by Manage Automatic Payments (F0770) is posted directly to the Main Bank G/L account.

➂Configuration (Test Setup) :

For testing, I created a new house bank and maintained the bank account determination so that House Bank Account ID: 101045 is assigned to G/L 11001000 – Main Bank Account (Cash/Bank) in Set Up Bank Determination for Payment Transactions.

④Test:

After running Manage Automatic Payments (F0770), the payment posting still uses G/L 11001020 – Bank Transfer (Domestic/SEPA/Foreign) (Cash/S) instead of G/L 11001000.

⑤Attachments:

Please refer to the attached screenshots showing the configuration and the test results.

Could you please advise

entry.title: Since the WHT exemption period has expired, a warning or error message should appear during the F110
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

At the time of supplier invoice creation, the vendor was eligible for a 100% withholding tax exemption. The withholding tax is calculated at the time of payment.

Based on the agreed payment terms, we processed the payment one month later; however, by then, the vendor's withholding tax exemption had expired. Consequently, the system calculated and deducted the Withholding Tax (WHT) during the payment run (F110).

We need a warning or error message stating that the withholding tax exemption has expired at the payment run (F110).

How do I do this configuration? Please guide me.

This is for the Israeli customer.

Thanks & Regards,

DNR

entry.title: Error while Uploading Camt53 for Croatia
entry.content: Hi Support, we want to upload the Croatian electronic bank statement to SAP by using the format Croatian CAMT.053.001.08. We assume that the error is caused by a missing electronic sequence number in the statement file. But the Croatian format in SAP should be configurated to use the legal sequence number not the electronic sequence number. Please check my attached screenshots. We are using SAP S/4 HANA public cloud 3-SL.

entry.title: Unable to confirm putaway task with change bin or Exception
entry.content:
Hi Support team,

I'm unable to change bin of putaway task during putaway task confirmation in foreground, there error message reflects, that is attached in same attachments.  

We are using CHBD exception code , 

Please let us know how to confirm foreground putaway task with change bin and split task in foreground.

 

Regards,

Pravesh Singh

entry.title: Can't find Unit Price in "Create Outgoing Invoices" App
entry.content: When creating an outgoing invoice, we are unable to find a field or option to update/maintain the Unit Price in the invoice line items.

entry.title: Cannot delete fields from Z* incompletion procedure (SD) – delete disabled / change not saved
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support,

We are configuring SD Incompletion Procedures and we face an issue with a custom (Z2) incompletion procedure.

Problem:
When we try to remove/delete specific fields from the Z2 incompletion procedure (copied/created as a customer procedure), the system does not allow the deletion. The delete option is either disabled (greyed out) or the entry reappears after saving / the system prevents saving without the field. As a result, we cannot adjust the incompletion log behavior for our business requirement.

Expected behavior:
We should be able to delete/remove fields from a customer-defined incompletion procedure (Z2 -Sales Item lvl) using the standard configuration activity." Define Incompleteness Procedures " in the CBC

Actual behavior:
Fields cannot be deleted from the Z2 procedure (delete is disabled).

Business impact:
Our sales/billing users are blocked by incomplete-document warnings/errors for fields that should not be mandatory in the new process, which prevents smooth document creation and testing.

Thank you.
Best regards,

entry.title: Account Assignment Category Q not available PR item level flexible workflow
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Account Assignment Category Q not available PR item level flexible workflow. Please check enclosed and let us know why Q is missing ? Provide details on how to get this category ?

entry.title: How to Completely Delete an Employee/Worker from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have read SAP KBA 2702011 - “Deleting Employee and Contingent Worker Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition” (2702011 - Deleting Employee and Contingent Worker Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition), but I am still unclear about the exact operational steps required to delete an Employee/Worker from the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system, especially from the Manage Workforce app.

Please guide me step by step on how to delete a Worker from the system.

If possible, screenshots or visual illustrations would be greatly appreciated.

 

Thank you for your answer.

QuocDT5

entry.title: Is it possible to disable document splitting in 2602
entry.content:
Dear all,

In 2602, document splitting is activated default, but I don't document splitting on profit center wise. Is it possible to disable document splitting in 2602? is there any Note or official document mention this?Thanks

B,R
